title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Authorities are investigating a student at Sandwich Middle High School for making a specific threat against two classmates.
The threat was detected by the school's safety app, GoGuardian, which flagged language on a school-issued device.
The student involved is not attending school during the investigation by Sandwich Police and Cape & Islands District Attorney’s Office.

title: Man convicted after breaking into the wrong Boston apartment and murdering resident
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Victor Arrington, 40, was found guilty of first-degree murder in the 2015 Dorchester home invasion and faces a life sentence.
Arrington and accomplices mistakenly broke into Richard Long's apartment, leading to Long's murder and attempted murder of his fiancée.
Prosecutors used Arrington's iPhone location data at retrial; a third suspect was never charged due to his unrelated death.

title: Some 20 New Englanders are on ‘American Ninja Warrior’ this season. Here’s your in-depth preview.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Season 18 of 'American Ninja Warrior' features 18 participants from New England, with 12 from Massachusetts.
New England ninja gyms, such as Vitality Obstacle Fitness, contribute to the region's strength in the sport.
Addy Herman and Noah Meunier, a couple and top competitors, are notable New Englanders competing this season.

title: Scotland came to Boston. Did romance follow?
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Scottish soccer fans visited Boston for the World Cup, creating a lively and friendly atmosphere in the city.
Local restaurant The Haven hosted Scottish cultural events, including a night where fans watched Scotland play Morocco.
Scottish visitors claimed loyalty in relationships, stating single men would declare their status, while coupled men remained faithful.

title: 4 of the weirdest, most absurd soccer movies streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Shaolin Soccer' combines soccer with Shaolin kung fu, gaining cult status in Hong Kong and the U.S. (Paramount+, Tubi, Pluto TV, Roku)
'Diamantino' is a political satire featuring a Ronaldo-esque star, addressing current relatable topics comedically. (Kanopy, Hoopla)
'United Passions', despite its notable cast, is infamous for its poor reception, grossing $918 on opening weekend. (Tubi, Roku, Plex, CW)

title: Is the Trump administration on the verge of further endangering North Atlantic right whales?
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The Trump administration is considering removing maritime slow zones that protect North Atlantic right whales from vessel strikes.
The North Atlantic right whale population, currently estimated at about 380, faces threats from human activities, particularly vessel strikes.
NOAA received over 29,000 responses during a public comment period on potential changes to vessel speed rules.

title: Payton Tolle gets standing ovation after tossing seven scoreless innings vs. Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Payton Tolle pitched seven scoreless innings for the Red Sox against the Yankees, receiving a standing ovation at Fenway Park.
Tolle, battling an illness, struck out seven and allowed just one hit, holding a perfect game into the sixth inning.
Tolle's season ERA is 2.78 over 12 starts, demonstrating prowess particularly against the Yankees with 18 strikeouts in two games.

title: 5 ways Celtics could improve lineup without trading Jaylen Brown
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Celtics are exploring ways to improve their roster while retaining Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum.
Boston could trade Derrick White and Baylor Scheierman for Trey Murphy and Yves Missi from the Pelicans.
Offloading Sam Hauser's contract may offer the Celtics more flexibility for new signings or trades.

title: 5 things to know about new Bruins forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins acquired forward JJ Peterka from the Utah Mammoth, trading two first-round picks in the process.
Peterka, known for his speed and scoring ability, has averaged 26 goals per season over the last three NHL seasons.
Originally from Munich, Germany, Peterka has represented his country internationally, including at the 2026 Winter Olympics.

title: NBA Rumor Roundup: The latest reports on the Jaylen Brown trade offers
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics are considering trade offers for Jaylen Brown, with interest from Portland and Detroit reported.
Portland Trail Blazers, seeking young talent and draft picks, reportedly interested in trading for Jaylen Brown.
Detroit Pistons consider a sign-and-trade involving Jalen Duren and Jaylen Brown to enhance their roster options.
